hero.mobileSubtitle: A joint engine combining memory scheduling with knowledge graph diagnostics. From your practice history, it schedules reviews at the question level and identifies weak knowledge clusters at the structural level.
hero.desktopSubtitle: Memorix-Field consists of two cooperating modules: a memory engine that tracks per-question forgetting curves to schedule reviews, and a graph diagnostic engine that models the entire subject knowledge graph to infer mastery across all knowledge points from sparse test results.

memorixDialog.title: Memorix-Field: 2nd-Gen Assessment Engine
memorixDialog.description: Memorix-Field extends spaced repetition with global knowledge graph diagnostics. Not a replacement, but a completion — scaling from per-item memory management to structural knowledge diagnosis.
memorixDialog.p1: Memory Engine — per-question forgetting modeling. Continuously fits the memory decay curve of each question, estimating individualized parameters at the question level. Intervenes precisely at the retrieval threshold to reduce wasted repetition and maximize retention per unit time. This is the core capability from Memoix Gen 1, now serving as the scheduling foundation in Gen 2.
memorixDialog.p2: Graph Diagnostic Engine — global knowledge graph inference. Models domain knowledge as a Gaussian Markov Random Field (GMRF), exploiting the smoothness prior that neighboring knowledge points share similar mastery levels. From partial observations — an exam covering 10% of knowledge points — it extrapolates mastery estimates for the entire graph, producing a full heatmap.
memorixDialog.p3: Dual-engine synergy. The memory engine outputs when to review which question. The graph engine outputs which knowledge clusters have structural weaknesses. The former optimizes time allocation; the latter optimizes knowledge construction. Both share a unified student model, with diagnostic results weighting scheduling priorities.
